---
description: "Use when working on localization, translations, XLIFF files, multilingual UI, Localized string, SET DATABASE LOCALIZATION, or .lproj folders."
applyTo: "{**/*.xlf,**/Localization/**,**/*.lproj/**,**/*.4dm}"
---

# 4D Localization & XLIFF

## Architecture

- Translations live in `Resources/<lang>.lproj/` folders (e.g., `en.lproj/`, `fr.lproj/`)
- Language codes follow ISO 639-1: `en`, `fr`, `de`, `es`, etc.
- `SET DATABASE LOCALIZATION($lang)` selects the active `.lproj` folder
- `Localized string($key)` looks up `resname` attribute in loaded XLIFF files
- Returns `""` (and `OK=0`) when key not found — always provide a fallback

## XLIFF File Rules

| Rule | Detail |
|------|--------|
| Extension | **Must be `.xlf`** — 4D ignores `.xliff` |
| Standard | XLIFF 1.2 |
| Case | `resname` lookup is **case-sensitive** |
| Multiple files | Allowed per folder; loaded in alphabetical order |
| Reload | Automatic when 4D comes to front and file modification date changed |

### Required Structure

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xliff version="1.2" xmlns="urn:oasis:names:tc:xliff:document:1.2">
  <file source-language="en" target-language="fr" datatype="plaintext" original="AppName">
    <body>
      <trans-unit id="1" resname="section.key">
        <source>English text</source>
        <target>Translated text</target>
      </trans-unit>
    </body>
  </file>
</xliff>
```

### Common Pitfalls

- **Never append** content after `</xliff>` — causes XML parser error (xmlc error 3)
- **One root `<xliff>` element** per file — no concatenated documents
- **`target-language` must match** the `.lproj` folder (e.g., `fr.lproj/` → `target-language="fr"`)
- **Escape `&` as `&amp;`** in `<source>` and `<target>` values
- **`id` attribute** is for internal reference only; `Localized string()` uses **`resname`**

## Key Naming Convention

Use dot-separated hierarchical keys: `{screen}.{element}` or `{screen}.{group}.{element}`

```
welcome.appName
welcome.feature.explorer.title
nav.explorer
settings.ai.provider
explorer.filters.region
```

## Automatic Translation via `:xliff:` References (Preferred)

4D natively resolves XLIFF references in form object titles. **No code needed per label.**

1. In the form JSON, use `:xliff:resname` as the object `"text"` value:
   ```json
   { "type": "text", "text": ":xliff:welcome.subtitle", "name": "lblSubtitle" }
   ```
2. Call `SET DATABASE LOCALIZATION($lang)` **before** opening the form
3. 4D automatically resolves `:xliff:welcome.subtitle` → translated `<target>` text

### Buttons with Emojis

The `:xliff:` reference must be at the **start** of the `"text"` value. For buttons that include emoji prefixes, include the emoji in the XLIFF value itself:

```xml
<trans-unit id="83" resname="explorer.btn.execute">
  <source>🔍  Execute Intelligence Match</source>
  <target>🔍  Lancer l'analyse sémantique</target>
</trans-unit>
```
```json
{ "type": "button", "text": ":xliff:explorer.btn.execute" }
```

### Runtime Language Switching

After `SET DATABASE LOCALIZATION($newLang)`, already-loaded forms keep their old language.

Two strategies:

1. **Re-navigate** — close and reopen the subform/page so 4D reloads with the new language (works for subforms loaded via `OBJECT SET SUBFORM`)
2. **Keep `_updateLabels()`** — for forms that switch language live without closing (e.g., Welcome dialog, Settings). The function calls `Localized string()` + `OBJECT SET TITLE` to force-refresh. Do **not** call it on `On Load` (`:xliff:` handles initial load), only after `switchLanguage()`.

### Object Datasource Values (Tabs, Dropdowns)

`:xliff:` references only work in form JSON `"text"` properties — they do **not** resolve inside object datasource `values` collections built in 4D code. To localize tabs or dropdowns:

1. Add XLIFF entries for each value label
2. Build the `values` collection using `Localized string()` in the constructor
3. In `_updateLabels()`, rebuild `values` with fresh translations and update `currentValue`

```4d
// Constructor — initial load uses active language
var $vals : Collection:=[Localized string("screen.tab.first"); Localized string("screen.tab.second")]
This.myTab:={values: $vals; index: 0; currentValue: $vals[0]}

// _updateLabels() — called after switchLanguage(), NOT on On Load
This.myTab.values:=[Localized string("screen.tab.first"); Localized string("screen.tab.second")]
This.myTab.currentValue:=This.myTab.values[This.myTab.index]
```

### Programmatic Fallback

Use `OBJECT SET TITLE` only when `:xliff:` references aren't practical (dynamic keys, computed text, emoji+text composition in sidebar collapse):

```4d
var $text : Text := Localized string("welcome.subtitle")
OBJECT SET TITLE(*; "lblSubtitle"; $text)
```

- **NOT** `OBJECT SET VALUE` — that sets a data source value, not the displayed title
- `Localized string($key)` returns `""` when not found — always provide a fallback

## Adding a New Language

1. Create `Resources/<lang>.lproj/` folder
2. Copy an existing `.xlf` file into it
3. Update `target-language` attribute to match new language code
4. Translate all `<target>` elements
5. Validate XML: `xmllint --noout file.xlf`
